rulings.Case Title: JEANETTE PARRISH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 26, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant Lehigh Hanson, Inc.¿s motion to quash service of summons is granted pursuant to the September 30 stipulated order in the lead case of Paillet v. Crown Cork and Seal (No. 275807). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: TERRY DECARLO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 26, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2. Defendant Tosco Corp.¿s motion for summary adjudication of its seventh and twenty-ninth affirmative defenses is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 26, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Defendant Superior Boiler Works, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Mr. Metcalf¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 26, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Defendant Parker Hannifin Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Mr. Metcalf¿s deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Ay¿s declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Metcalf was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s hearsay objections to Mr. Metcalf¿s identification of Sacomo products based on seeing ¿Sacomo¿ on packaging and rope clamps are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 26, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Intervenors Hartford Fire Insurance Co., et al.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 26, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Defendant St. Francis Hotel Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Metcalf was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Metcalf¿s deposition testimony and responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 26, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Defendant Riley Power Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of the causes of action for negligence, strict liability and loss of consortium are denied. Defendant did not sustain its initial burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Metcalf was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 26, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Defendant Wickes Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of the causes of action for negligence, strict liability and loss of consortium are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production but Dr. Horn¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Metcalf¿s exposure to asbestos-containing Hamilton joint compound attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his risk of developing mesothelioma. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 26, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Defendant The Olympic Glove & Safety Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. D¿Amico and its progeny do not make Mr. Metcalf¿s deposition testimony, discovery responses or declaration inadmissible in this action based on his prior declaration in the Simpson case. Mr. Metcalf¿s deposition testimony and declaration in this action create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (asbestos gloves and blankets) attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 26, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Defendant PCC Flow Technologies LP¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar. Mr. Metcalf¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (gaskets) attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs represent that they are dismissing their cause of action for false representation and claim for punitive damages and those were the only issues defendant sought to have summarily adjudicated. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: IVAN MERL BARNEY VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 26, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5. Defendant Kelly Moore Company¿s motion to strike Plaintiff¿s request for punitive damages is denied. Plaintiff¿s complaint alleges sufficient facts to support a finding that D acted with fraud, oppression, or malice and meets the requirements of CCP § 3294. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)
